D1 Optics L3 Apply Reference

Newton's Rings

press a lens onto flat glass and read the dark-ring radii of the air wedge

The air gap under a curved lens grows as r²/2R. A half-wave phase flip at the lower glass makes the contact point dark, with rings where the round-trip path equals whole wavelengths.

Set the lens radius of curvature R and the wavelength. Toggle white light to see the coloured Newton's rings. The gold readout gives the first dark-ring radius.
